title: campanula sibirica subsp. charkeviczii
intro: Campanula sibirica subsp. charkeviczii, also known as the Siberian bellflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Campanulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Campanula sibirica subsp. charkeviczii include reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or decoctions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
